title: Chocolate drip finish
subtitle: Ganache dripped around the edge of a frosted cake for a controlled cascade
excerpt: The drip finish uses a slightly thin ganache poured around the top edge of a frosted cake, where it runs down the side in irregular drops. The drip length is controlled by ganache temperature: cooler ganache makes shorter, thicker drips; warmer makes longer, thinner ones. The cake must be chilled before the ganache goes on.
sourceNotes: Ganache as a cake finish is documented in French confectionery manuals from the nineteenth century; its use as a drip or cascade over a frosted layer cake is a contemporary domestic adaptation. The underlying ganache technique (cream and chocolate combined in a fixed ratio) appears in Mrs A.B. Marshall's confectionery works and in domestic baking guides of the late nineteenth century. The drip-finish application is now widely reproduced in domestic baking references and is fully public-domain.

content.text: The drip finish is the most forgiving of the chocolate decorating techniques. Irregular drips look intentional; only uniform drips that have run too far to the base look like an accident. Test on the back of the cake first.

Heat the {{double-cream}} in a small saucepan until it just begins to simmer. Pour over the chopped {{dark-chocolate}} in a bowl. Leave for 2 minutes, then stir from the centre outward until the ganache is smooth and glossy. Leave to cool to 32 to 35°C.
Refrigerate the frosted cake for 30 minutes until the buttercream or frosting is completely firm. Check the

: it should flow slowly from the spoon. Using a teaspoon or a piping bottle, place small drops of ganache around the top edge at intervals of about 3 cm, nudging each drop gently toward the edge with the spoon so it starts to drip down. Vary the starting distance from the edge to vary the drip length.

Once all the edge drips are set (about 5 minutes in the fridge), pour the remaining ganache onto the top of the cake and smooth with the icing spatula. Return to the fridge for 10 minutes before adding any further decoration.

recipe.yieldDescription: enough for 1 drip finish on a 20 cm cake
